name: python3-dunamai
title: python3-dunamai
description:
dynamic versioning library and CLI
Dunamai is a Python library and command line tool for producing dynamic,
standards-compliant version strings, derived from tags in your version control
system. This facilitates uniquely identifying nightly or per-commit builds in
continuous integration and releasing new versions of your software simply by
creating a tag.

arguments.raw: --format  Custom output format. Available substitutions: {base}, {stage}, {revision},

arguments.raw: --full-commit  Get the full commit hash instead of the short form (only: Git, Mercurial)

arguments.raw: --no-metadata  Never include metadata. Ignored when --format is used

arguments.raw: --strict  Elevate warnings to errors. When there are no tags, fail instead of falling back

arguments.raw: --style  format. Will default to PEP 440 if not set and no custom

arguments.raw: --tag-dir  Location of tags relative to the root (only: Subversion)

arguments.description: DUNAMAI(1)
arguments.raw: -07-04  DUNAMAI(1)

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dunamai because dynamic versioning library and CLI.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dunamai as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dunamai [-h] {from,check} ...` as documented upstream for dunamai.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dunamai with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dunamai --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
